domain: Mantis shrimp 16-channel cone color vision (12-16 photoreceptor types, 300-720nm + polarization, channel-independent encoding with delayed cross-channel comparison at higher levels)
specific_mechanism: Narrow-band parallel photoreceptor channels operate independently within the retina; color information is encoded by channel identity rather than cross-channel ratio comparison; downstream analytical load is reduced because each channel pre-discretizes its spectral slice.
llm_application: STOMATOPOD-HEAD: replace standard multi-head attention with K=16 narrow-band specialist sub-heads, each operating on a disjoint precomputed spectral slice (e.g., DCT bin) of token-positional content. No intra-layer cross-channel comparison — each sub-head produces an independent output that is concatenated and only fed to the next layer's aggregator. Hypothesis: reduces inter-head redundancy and per-channel softmax-temperature variance.
novelty_claim: GQA/MQA share K/V across heads (different mechanism). MoA tailors sparse configurations per head but does not impose frequency-disjoint specialization. Gated DeltaNet gates across heads but mixes across channels. Spectral attention variants (e.g., FNet, FFT-based) replace softmax entirely with FFT, not narrow-band specialists. STOMATOPOD-HEAD's distinctive contribution is *frequency-disjoint specialists with no intra-layer cross-channel comparison*.
